body: SpyneJS organizes code around three basic items found in most frontend applications — View, Behavior, and Logic, described here as the VBL pattern.

The framework combines VBL with familiar tools and the practical integration of reactive and functional patterns — to provide an intuitive system for syncronizing real DOM elements to events and data.
This structure creates highly performant applications that are efficient to build — and generates codebases that remain coherent and maintanable regardless of size.

AI Collaboration — SpyneJS' clear mental models gives developer's control and understanding of their applications, allowing AI collaboration to focus on precise integration of well-organzed and clean, generative code.
ViewStream — The View in VBL, focused on declarative wiring

block.footnote: You can see the examples section for more advanced examples of ViewStream.

block.title: View, Behavor and Logic Classes
block.subtitle: Main functions and setup of SpyneJS VBL Classes

block.title: SpyneJS Application Structure
block.footer: See the VBL Hello World Example

body: See the VBL Hello World Example

Where to Go Next?
Dive in to examples, start building right way, or continue on to learn the basic steps of intializing a Spyne application:

  • Explore More Examples → See how SpyneJS powers apps like a Meme Generator, Todos, and single-page flows.
  • Start Building → Learn how to generate a SpyneJS app with the Application Generator, and the Command Line Tool.
  • Go Further → Explore advanced examples and patterns in References.
Applications are driven by events — triggered by Users, Browsers, and JavaScript APIs.
SpyneJS captures all event-driven behaviors with three dedicated Event Channels — UI, WINDOW, and ROUTE channels.
These Event channels are loaded when SpyneApp is initialized.
They emit behavior using the same consistent, declarative ChannelPayload interface used across the Channel system — making event streams easy to observe, wire, and test.

SpyneJS Event Channels at a Glance These three Event Channels are automatically created when SpyneApp is initialized.
Channel Event Source Common Use Cases How Events Are Declared
UI Channel DOM events (click, input) Button clicks, form submissions, inputs ViewStream.broadcastEvents()
Route Channel Location & history API SPA navigation, deep linking, back/forward ViewStream.broadcastEvents()
Window Channel Global browser events Resize, scroll, media queries, visibility Configuration
The Event Channel streams can be consumed by ViewStream instances, and they can be subscribed to or merged with other streams using custom Channels.
For implementation details, including event configuration and usage patterns, see the Reference, Configure, and Examples sections.

block.heading:
SPA Behavior and the Route Channel:
SpyneJS enables single-page navigation to be controlled entirely through data.

The Route Channel manages navigation and back/forward history using a route map configuration object — allowing it to both generate links and emit structured routeData on location changes.

This allows application navigation to be generated dynamically, including by AI-Assisted tools.

body: Creating a SpyneJS application starts with three essential steps:
Use the SpyneApp.init() method to create a new Spyne application instance:
This configuration json object can include global app settings and defines options for the Window and Route Channels.
Explore the configuration UI to generate a full config object.
After initializing the app, register your custom Channels and optional Plugins.
The following example demonstrates registering a custom channel and loading a SpyneJS  plugin:
Every SpyneJS app begins with at least one ViewStream attached to the DOM.
This root ViewStream serves as the entry point for rendering and can host other nested ViewStreams.

block.heading: Understanding SpyneJS for Angular Developers
block.subheading1: The journey of creating an application usually begins with the same general steps:

body:
What is Familar to Angular Developers
If you’ve used Angular’s declarative templates, component-based structure, and services to handle behavior — much of that mental model will feel immediately familiar in SpyneJS.

SpyneJS offers a similar separation of concerns — but in a lighter, more modular system that doesn’t require decorators, complex DI, or boilerplate-heavy lifecycle management.

What You Gain with SpyneJS
SpyneJS offers a streamlined structure that makes logic and behavior easier to see, test, and evolve.

Rather than scattering logic across services, decorators, and reactive forms, SpyneJS places reusable logic in traits that work seamlessly with both views and channels — keeping behavior clear, testable, and easy to evolve.

SpyneJS simplifies what Angular made powerful — giving you the same capabilities with fewer abstractions and more clarity.

COMPARISON — Tour of Heroes
Angular’s classic single-page app example — recreated in SpyneJS using modular views, traits, and a channel-driven behavior system.

The example demonstrates routing, UI updates, event handling, and modular component reuse — all using the SpyneJS VBL structure.

block.heading: Understanding SpyneJS for React Developers
block.subheading: The journey of creating an application usually begins with the same general steps:

body:
What is Familar to React Developers
If you’ve built components with JSX, worked with props and state, or used hooks to manage local behavior, you’ll feel right at home in SpyneJS. The concepts of declarative views and composable UI are shared — but in SpyneJS, they’re paired with a clearer separation of logic and behavior.

SpyneJS lets you build with similar mental models, while giving you more control over how behavior flows and where logic lives.

What You Gain with SpyneJS
React gives you great flexibility — but as applications grow, that flexibility often leads to fragmented behavior and logic co-mingled with markup.

SpyneJS solves this by organizing logic into reusable traits and directing all behavior through declarative channels.

Rather than juggling hooks, context, and prop-passing, you define logic once and use it anywhere — without wrapping or restructuring your view. Views stay declarative, behavior is centralized, and everything responds reactively by design.

For many developers, this shift doesn’t feel limiting — it feels like clarity they didn’t know they were missing.
COMPARISON — Tic-Tac-Toe Tutorial
Based on React’s classic starter example, this version of Tic-Tac-Toe is implemented in SpyneJS with separated logic, observable behavior, and a clean, declarative view.
This isn’t a full SPA — React doesn’t provide an official single-page-app example. But this tutorial serves as a useful reference for developers beginning to “think in SpyneJS.”
It shows how logic, behavior, and view structure evolve naturally as the application grows.

SpyneJS was designed to feel familiar to developers coming from other modern frameworks, while offering a clearer structure that aligns with how developers naturally reason about application behavior.
If you’ve ever built components, used a templating system, or managed state, you should feel immediately capable in SpyneJS.
Most of your existing frontend knowledge carries over — but now it’s mapped into a declarative, observable framework where logic and behavior are clearly defined.
And that clarity has a purpose: in SpyneJS, code becomes a readable roadmap — not just for developers, but for AI-assisted tools as well.

block.heading:
The SpyneJS AI Advantage
By cleanly separating logic, presentation, and behavior, SpyneJS creates a structure that both developers and AI can read, reason about, and extend.

Future versions will introduce a metadata layer and MCP-compatible App Generator, enabling AI tools to contribute code, logic, and structure with confidence — just like a developer would.

The upcoming SpyneJS AI Assistant will help visualize and wire applications across Views, Behavior, and Logic, making the framework uniquely suited for collaborative, AI-driven development.

body: Traits, Channels, and ViewStreams offer a structure that’s both powerful and easy to extend — not in spite of its simplicity, but because of it.

You may find that SpyneJS feels “too easy” at first — and that’s the point. A framework should make building easier, not more complex. With SpyneJS, the hard part is no longer wiring the app — it’s imagining the experience you want to create.

block.heading:
“Thinking in SpyneJS”:
  • Views are organized into a clear hierarchy
  • Behavior is gathered and emitted through channels
  • Logic lives in reusable traits
SpyneJS encourages you to declare structure, styles, and behavior cleanly — then wire it all reactively, without losing readability.

block.title: ViewStream
block.subtitle: Dynamically generates the DOM by nesting ViewStream instances.
bullets: Defines HTML elements and content
Appends to DOM  or nests ViewStream instances
Built in el$ Selector
Encapsulated and communicates with all Channels
Imports native methods from SpyneTraits

block.title: DomElement
block.subtitle: Creates a DOM element and populates it with HTML or text content, using the same underlying logic as a ViewStream.
bullets: DomElement is used to create ViewStreams HTML element and its content
Can be used to create HTML elements that do not require integration with Channels and SpyneTraits

block.title: DomElementTemplate
block.subtitle: A flexible system for generating HTML content using Templates and Data—primarily used for rendering HTML within ViewStreams and DomElements.
bullets: This generates HTML from templates and data.
Fastest way to generate large amounts of DOM Content without any logic or code
Can be rendered as an HTML String or DocumentFragement

block.title: Channel
block.subtitle: Channels provide a unified interface to manage and synchronize application behavior, including user interactions, browser activities, and data fetching.
bullets: Unified system to transmit all types of events and data
Combines and parses data from other channels
Create advanced observables using rxjs library

block.title: ChannelPayload
block.subtitle: This provides a consistent interface to efficiently deliver behavior content.
bullets: This data interface for all channels
Provides consistent, immutable data

block.title: ChannelPayloadFilter
block.subtitle: Filters allow ViewStream and Channel instances to reactively connect to specific  ChannelPayloads.
bullets: Used by custom Channel and ViewStream instances to filter just the requreid Channel Payloads

block.title: ChannelFetch
block.subtitle:
Integrates the native Fetch API into SpyneJS’s Channel platform.
Standardizes API communication, enabling CRUD operations and external data integration within the Channel system.
bullets: Creates a channel using content returned from the Fetch API
Can be used as CRUD connection to external APIs and services

block.title: ChannelFetchUtil
block.subtitle:
A utility class that powers ChannelFetch.
Provides a consistent, composable interface for configuring and executing Fetch API requests as observables.
bullets: This utiliity is used by Channel Fetch to generate observables from the Fetch API
Can be added to ChannelFetch modules to create sophisticated APIs

block.title: SpyneTrait
block.subtitle: Encapsulates reusable logic as pure functions, easily bindable to ViewStream or Channel instances.
bullets: Organizes logic for codebase
Convenient system to test pure functions, that are automatically composed to instances

block.title: SpyneApp
block.subtitle: Initializes the SpyneJS application and applies configuration settings.
bullets: Creates the Channel Pipeline on intialization
Takes in the configuration file for channels and other options
Registers Channels so that they can be access globally

block.title: SpyneAppProperties
block.subtitle: This is a "Singleton" that can be used to store temporary properties, and to retrieve configuration and navlinks.
bullets: Creates the Channel Pipeline on intialization
Takes in the configuration file for channels and other options
Registers Channels so that they can be access globally

block.title: SpynePlugin
block.subtitle: In early beta, SpynePlugin allows for third-party plugins to be created
bullets: Allows developers to create shareable code and components

block.title: ViewStream Class
block.subtitle: ViewStream is the main view class

body:
Application Builder (AI-Powered, Alpha Stage)
SpyneJS can generate a fully structured single-page application based on a simple configuration — with content generated using OpenAI models and images from Pexels.

How to Generate a Single-Page-Application:
  1. Configure a Menu
    Add pages and cards using the Routes Generator.
    This structure will guide how the AI models generate content and images.
  2. Log In and Click “Create App”
    Create or log in to your account and click the Create App button.
  3. Preview the Application
    Click Preview App to launch your new site at preview.spynejs.com
    You can immediately explore and test your app at preview.spynejs.org — no local setup required.

The current system is in alpha, but it’s built on a foundation that will support:

  • Integration with style frameworks, commerce platforms, animation libraries, and external CMS/database services
  • Editorial content pipelines (not just marketing-focused generation)
  • Multiple hosting options and theme customization
  • AI-guided refinement and expansion via the upcoming SpyneJS AI Assistant

As the framework evolves, this tool will serve as a complete App Generation UI — giving developers and AI assistants a shared space to design, structure, and deploy applications from a central schema.

The current output is a foundational scaffold — functionally minimal and visually basic — intended for testing, feedback, and gaining familiarity with SpyneJS-based development.

block.title: Configuration  Tool
block.subtitle: SpyneJS applications rely on configuration to define options, plugins, behavior and routing.

block.title: Spyne is a complete system of tools and patterns for solving the most difficult front end challenges.

Real DOM, pure JavaScript framework, easy to get started building applicaitons
Optimized for performance and memory management
Channel system simplifies use of powerful rxjs observable tools
ViewStream familar collection of features with unique ability to render, synchronize and remove with other UI elements
ROUTE window path location as data
Integrate APIs with ease using Fetch Channels
Immutable data and encapsulated ViewStream instances makes development efficient

content.name: intro
content.title:
SpyneJS: The First Frontend Architecture for
Developer & AI Collaboration

block.heading: The only system where humans and models share a common structure — View, Behavior, Logic.

body:
THE MARKET OPPORTUNITY A Massive and Fast-Growing Market
Frontend is the universal bottleneck — every SaaS product, enterprise portal, and consumer app depends on it.
Investors outside the space often underestimate just how large and urgent this market has become:
  • Application Development Software: $258B in 2024 → $863B by 2030 (22.8% CAGR)
  • Web Development: $55B in 2024 → $101B by 2034 (6.3% CAGR)
  • AI in Software Development: $674M in 2024 → $15.7B by 2033 (42.3% CAGR)
  • AI Code Tools: $4.3B in 2023 → $12.6B by 2028 (24% CAGR)
SpyneJS sits at the intersection: the $100B+ frontend layer and the $15B+ AI developer-tools layer — solving the exact bottleneck that makes AI code brittle and unscalable.

THE PROBLEMThe Bottleneck That’s Been Holding Back the Web — and AI
Frontend logic has been a mess — limiting browser capabiliities and forcing AI to make sense of scattered UI components.
Legacy frameworks weren’t built for clean AI integration. And they trained dev teams to work around inconsistent and confusing structures.
  • Shallow UI became the default — not by design, but by limitation
  • Core behavior and logic are scattered, brittle, and hard to evolve
  • AI is now mass-producing the same mess — faster, not better
  • Tech debt shows up early and spreads quickly
  • Enterprise teams are trapped in MVP mode — even at full scale
SpyneJS breaks the false tradeoff between speed and control — giving AI and developers a shared structure they can build on, cleanly and scalably.

The Real Risk Behind AI Codegen
The hype hides a deeper structural flaw.

For over a decade, the web has been held back by disorganized frontend code — logic tangled with layout, behavior scattered across components. It’s that same architecture AI is now replicating at scale.
  • Enterprise teams can't work with black boxes — they need structure and control
  • Codegen alone pressures teams to trade visibility for velocity
  • AI is accelerating the mess — because all it’s learned from is unstructured code
SpyneJS gives AI a structured system for clean code — and developers a mental model to understand and evolve AI-collaborative apps.

THE SOLUTIONA New Development Format
A new frontend foundation, built for the AI era.
Framework Layer Traditional Frameworks SpyneJS
Structure Mixed logic and UI inside components Strict separation of View, Behavior, and Logic (VBL)
Behavior Buried in event handlers and lifecycle methods Routed through observable Channels with clear payloads
AI Integration Raw codegen with no structure or intent AI generates Traits, Templates, and Styles within defined architecture
Developer Control Manual overrides and scattered wiring Intent-driven wiring with testable, observable streams
Collaboration One-way scaffolds and limited reusability Shared architecture readable by both humans and AI
How It Works

SpyneJS structures the frontend layer where that bottleneck lives — turning ambiguity into modular, observable architecture.
  • View = Composable ViewStreams → nested, declarative layout
  • Behavior = Shared Channels → observable event streams
  • Logic = Pure SpyneTraits → reusable, testable functions

The VBL architecture introduces a new application format: one readable by AI, traceable by developers, and powerful enough to structure the next generation of web experiences.

The VBL architecture is a particularly thoughtful approach to tackling the technical debt created by AI-generated code, which is a challenge we're hearing more and more about.
— Erik Nordlander, General Partner, GV
Why AI Codegen Works Better with SpyneJS

SpyneJS teaches AI to generate structured code — not just code that “runs.”
  • Faster generation — AI fills well-defined VBL units, not raw boilerplate
  • No tech debt — output is modular, testable, and ready for scale
  • Reusable logic — traits and channels keep behavior consistent across the app
  • Observable behavior — every interaction is visible and debuggable
  • Human-level clarity — devs can trace logic instantly, even in AI-generated flows
Product Snapshot

The developer experience is clean, fast, and scalable.
  • App Builder
    Configure layout, structure, and content with AI support
  • Spyne CLI
    Scaffold entire VBL apps with modular, production-grade code
  • CMS
    Edit content and generate code in-browser with AI-assisted UI
  • Schematic Viewer (coming soon)
    Visual AI collaboration through a live schematic of your app’s architecture
Full docs, working CMS, and live examples are available now.
GO-TO-MARKETStrategy

Now – Developer Activation
  • CLI, docs, and CMS preview are live
  • Outreach via Reddit, Hacker News, niche dev communities

Fall – AI Assistant Rollout
  • Launch App Builder with CLI + AI prompt integration
  • Publish demo video and technical deep-dives
  • Position SpyneJS as the structured alternative to AI spaghetti code

Winter – Commercial Launch
  • Release hosted App Builder + CMS
  • Launch paid subscriptions with AI editing, themes, plugin scaffolds

Spring 2026 – Professional Developer Expansion
  • VSCode and IntelliJ plugins
  • Public plugin system for logic, themes, and CMS adapters
  • Support enterprise pilots and monetize plugin ecosystem
Business Model

SpyneJS is the framework and the tools around it.
  • Free tier for developers
  • Paid plans for CLI prompts, CMS access, hosted App Builder
  • Plugin marketplace + licensing
  • Enterprise plans with team workflows, hosting, and devtool access
  • CLI and CMS drive bottom-up adoption. Plugins and hosted tools unlock monetization.
  • Over time, SpyneJS becomes the structured IDE layer for modern SaaS.
Roadmap & Use of Funds

SpyneJS is already in production use.
  • Framework, CMS, and CLI are live
  • Next milestones:
    • App Builder public release
    • Schematic Viewer
    • Plugin system + CMS AI prompt support

Funding Use
  • 40% → Engineering + Design
  • 30% → Go-to-Market + Community
  • 15% → AI Infrastructure
  • 15% → Founder runway + ops
The Ask

We’re currently raising to accelerate the next phase of SpyneJS:
  • Launch App Builder and schematic viewer
  • Finalize AI–CMS collaboration flow
  • Bring structured AI apps to thousands of frontend developers
Founder

Frank Batista —Solo founder, architect, and developer
  • 20+ years in design systems and enterprise-scale apps
  • Built SpyneJS from real-world demands
  • Architecting the next generation of frontend tooling

The foundation is in place — with the right partner, we’ll scale faster and unlock the full potential of the AI-powered internet.
frank@spynejs.com
LinkedIn

We typically begin be creating a view file for each element of the app that requires logic.
The view files typically declares the elements attributes, such as class, and also renders the views DOM content by merging data with templates.
Views alone cannot do much, so the next step is to synchronize and transform views by wiring to the loading of events and the triggering of data.
Spyne is designed to match this mental process.
